name: DASA — UK Defence & Security Accelerator
overview: DASA is the UK Ministry of Defence's innovation hub: it finds and funds (usually 100% grant) novel ideas from companies — mostly SMEs — to solve defence and security problems, taking them from concept to prototype. AI runs through much of its work, most visibly its flagship 'Intelligent Ship' programme on human-AI teaming for warships, plus electromagnetic-warfare and autonomy competitions. Its public footprint is rich on competition documents, funding figures and named winners, though DASA is now being folded into the wider UK Defence Innovation (UKDI) reorganisation and its long-running Open Call is closing.

established.label: The MOD's innovation accelerator
established.plain: DASA was set up in 2016 as part of the UK Ministry of Defence to find and fund innovation that gives the armed forces and national security a battle-winning edge. It runs two main routes: themed competitions on specific problems, and a year-round Open Call for any disruptive idea. AI and autonomy have been priority themes from early on.

established.label: The always-open funnel
established.plain: The Open Call is DASA's standing front door: innovators can pitch any idea — concept, product or service, at any maturity — without waiting for a specific competition, with assessment cycles running through the year. Funding is typically £100k–£350k, often 100% grant, with projects aimed at reaching a working prototype (TRL 6–7). Around 60% of companies DASA funds are SMEs.
established.sourceName: GOV.UK — DASA Open Call for Innovation

established.label: £1m, 9 AI projects for warships
established.plain: DASA's flagship AI programme, run for Dstl, explores 'human-AI and AI-AI teaming' on naval platforms to cut crew information overload. Phase 1 in 2021 shared £1m across nine projects — including Rolls-Royce, Nottingham Trent University, Decision Lab, CGI, Frazer-Nash, Montvieux and SeeByte — to build early intelligent agents and a sandbox AI network (ISAIN).
established.sourceName: Naval Technology — DASA awards £3m for Intelligent Ship

established.label: £2m, 10 AI agents + an integrator
established.plain: Phase 2 (£2m) funded ten 'intelligent agents' plus an integrator to weave them into the Intelligent Ship AI Network and run a series of evaluation events. Named winners included SeeByte, Decision Lab, DIEM Analytics, Frazer-Nash, Montvieux, Nottingham Trent University, Rolls-Royce and CGI — moving the programme from components to an integrated, evaluated system.

established.label: AI for the EM spectrum
established.plain: Through its 'Invisible Battlespace' competition, DASA funded UK AI firm Massive Analytic to apply its 'Artificial Precognition' technique to electromagnetic operations — automatically recognising and classifying signal patterns and building cognitive radio so UK forces can sense, manage and exploit the EM spectrum in real time across multiple domains.
established.sourceName: Financial IT — Massive Analytic wins DASA competition

inProgress.label: Human-Autonomy Teams for C2
inProgress.plain: Phase 3 (up to £1.7m, co-funded with Dstl, launched April 2023) shifts focus to 'Human-Autonomy Teams' — designing how collaborative AI agents and human operators should work together for naval command and control, including how the system arbitrates between them. It targets requirements and design approaches for future warship C2.

inProgress.label: Fast-paced autonomy challenges
inProgress.plain: A rapid, multi-cycle themed competition (for Dstl) seeking exploitable autonomy and AI for future platforms. The 2024 run (£2m total, ~£50k–£200k per bid, open Jul–Dec 2024) asked for things like basic autonomy for legacy ground vehicles and new UAS navigation; a 2025 Phase Two added challenges including autonomous navigation for UAS and uncrewed surface vessels.

inProgress.label: The funnel is being reorganised
inProgress.plain: As the MOD stands up a wider UK Defence Innovation (UKDI) body to reach full operating capability, DASA's long-running Open Call for Innovation is being wound down: the final cycle (Cycle 4) was extended to 13 January 2026, after which the standing Open Call route closes. This marks a significant change to how the funnel will operate.

latest.label: 50+ funded suppliers on show
latest.plain: At DSEI 2025 (Sept 2025) DASA showcased over 50 funded suppliers, including AI ones such as NW PRO, whose AI-driven NexuSec security platform and SensorSync digital-twin scanning tech are being extended into defence. International visitors from Australia, Canada, Belgium and Singapore came to study DASA's rapid funding model.

suppliers.name: SeeByte
suppliers.role: funded company — Intelligent Ship Phase 1 & 2 (GALILEO / ADeM decision-making AI agent)
suppliers.claim: SeeByte says its DASA-funded GALILEO agent offers "goal-based mission decomposition, that integrates within the Intelligent Ship AI Network".

suppliers.name: Massive Analytic
suppliers.role: funded company — 'Invisible Battlespace' AI for electromagnetic operations ('Artificial Precognition')
suppliers.sourceName: Financial IT — Massive Analytic wins DASA competition

suppliers.name: Decision Lab
suppliers.role: funded company — Intelligent Ship Phase 1 & 2 AI agent
suppliers.sourceName: Naval Technology — DASA awards £3m for Intelligent Ship

suppliers.name: Rolls-Royce
suppliers.role: funded company — Intelligent Ship Phase 1 & 2 (power/propulsion decision-support AI)
suppliers.sourceName: Naval Technology — DASA awards £3m for Intelligent Ship

suppliers.name: NW PRO
suppliers.role: funded company — AI-driven NexuSec security platform & SensorSync digital-twin scanning (DSEI 2025)

sources.name: GOV.UK — DASA Open Call for Innovation
Naval Technology — DASA awards £3m for Intelligent Ship

unknowns: Individual funding amounts per Intelligent Ship winner, and the operational outcomes of those AI agents on actual Royal Navy vessels, are not public. Exactly how DASA's AI competitions and Open Call will be absorbed into the new UK Defence Innovation (UKDI) body — and which routes survive after the Open Call closes in January 2026 — is still being defined.
